agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over sensitive medical records, and immutability of clinical documentation after a defined window.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Patient","definition":"An individual registered on the platform who can book appointments, view their own medical records, and cancel their own appointments within the allowed window."},{"term":"Physician","definition":"A licensed medical professional who provides consultations. Must have a verified license before accepting any bookings."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"An internal platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments outside the patient-cancellable window."},{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled consultation between exactly one patient and one physician, defined by a start time and end time. Cannot overlap with another appointment for the same physician."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments for the same physician have overlapping time ranges. This is a forbidden state."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's medical license number before they are permitted to appear as bookable on the platform."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Become permanently read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Medical Record","definition":"Protected health information belonging to a patient, accessible only to the patient themselves and the physician who is the treating provider for the relevant appointment."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which the patient may self-cancel. After this window closes, only a Staff Administrator may cancel."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment, and therefore granted access to that patient's medical records for that consultation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient may only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing an unlicensed physician to accept bookings is a regulatory and patient-safety violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"Given a physician whose license_verified status is false, any attempt to create an appointment with that physician MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time ranges.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking violates physical reality — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. If this state exists, data integrity is compromised.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments may exist where appointment_A.start < appointment_B.end AND appointment_B.start < appointment_A.end."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 4 hours or 1 hour) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request MUST be rejected when current_time >= (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A Staff Administrator may cancel any appointment regardless of the cancellation window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy granting elevated privilege. The scope of admin cancellation authority is a policy decision.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a Staff Administrator MUST succeed for any appointment that is not already cancelled or completed, regardless of time until start."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before the physician can accept bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a compliance gate. Bypassing it means the platform is facilitating potentially unlicensed medical practice, a regulatory violation that corrupts the trust model.","testable_assertion":"A physician record with license_verified = false MUST NOT appear in bookable physician listings and MUST reject any appointment creation attempts."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data confidentiality invariant rooted in healthcare privacy regulations (analogous to HIPAA). Violation means unauthorized disclosure of protected health information — a fundamentally corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"Any request to access a patient's medical record MUST be denied unless the requester is the patient OR the treating physician for a relevant appointment."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become permanently read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment end time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 48-hour window is a policy-driven threshold. An organization could adjust this to 24 or 72 hours. However, the concept that notes eventually lock IS an integrity concern — the threshold itself is policy.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to modify consultation notes when current_time >= (appointment.end_time + 48 hours) MUST be rejected. Notes modified before the lock window MUST be persisted normally."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and no overlapping appointments","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The appointment is created successfully and both patient and physician can see it"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The system rejects the booking with a clear error indicating the physician is not available"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician who already has an appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A patient attempts to book that same physician from 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The system rejects the booking with an error indicating a scheduling conflict"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A physician who already has an appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A patient attempts to book that same physician from 10:30 to 11:00","then":"The appointment is created successfully because the time ranges do not overlap (boundary is non-overlapping)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with an appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A patient with an appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the patient that only a staff administrator can cancel at this point"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Staff Administrator and an appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"The administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with medical records and an appointment with Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Smith (the treating physician) requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"Access is granted and records are displayed"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A patient with medical records and an appointment with Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Jones (a different physician, NOT treating) requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"Access is denied"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment that ended 47 hours ago with associated consultation notes","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully because the 48-hour lock window has not elapsed"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An appointment that ended 49 hours ago with associated consultation notes","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected and the physician is informed the notes are now locked"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A patient viewing their own medical records","when":"The patient requests access","then":"The records are displayed to the patient"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"An appointment that ended exactly 48 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected — the boundary condition treats 48 hours as the lock point (greater-than-or-equal)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked AFTER appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged or cancelled by the system/administrator. The physician must be immediately removed from the bookable pool. Past completed appointments remain valid records."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to book at the exact boundary where another appointment ends (e.g., book 10:30 when prior ends at 10:30)","expected_behavior":"Booking should succeed — appointments sharing only a boundary point are not overlapping. The interval comparison must be half-open: [start, end)."},{"scenario":"Cancellation request arrives at exactly the 2-hour boundary before start time","expected_behavior":"The system must define boundary behavior explicitly. Recommendation: at exactly 2 hours before, patient cancellation is still allowed (strictly less-than comparison on the cutoff)."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes edited at exactly the 48-hour mark after appointment end","expected_behavior":"Notes should be locked at the 48-hour boundary. The >= comparison means edits at exactly 48 hours are rejected."},{"scenario":"Physician has back-to-back appointments and tries to access records of patient from a different appointment","expected_behavior":"Access is granted ONLY for the specific patient the physician is treating. Having any appointment does not grant access to all patients."},{"scenario":"Staff Administrator attempts to cancel an already-completed appointment","expected_behavior":"Cancellation should be rejected — a completed consultation cannot be retroactively cancelled. Only future or in-progress appointments are cancellable."},{"scenario":"Multiple concurrent booking requests for the same physician in the same time slot (race condition)","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce the no-double-booking invariant at the persistence layer (e.g., database constraint or serialized transaction). Only one booking succeeds; others receive a conflict error."},{"scenario":"Patient account is deactivated but medical records still exist","expected_behavior":"Medical records must be retained per regulatory requirements. Access rules still apply — treating physicians of past appointments may need continued access for continuity of care, subject to policy."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect double-booking wastes physician time and damages patient trust, leading to churn. Failure to verify licenses exposes the platform to legal liability and potential fines. Locked consultation notes protect against malpractice disputes.","operational":"Scheduling integrity is foundational — every downstream workflow (notifications, billing, prescriptions) depends on a clean appointment state. License verification is a one-time gate per physician but blocks onboarding if not automated. The 48-hour note-locking window requires a reliable clock/timer mechanism.","user":"Patients expect a seamless booking experience with no conflicts. The cancellation window policy balances patient flexibility against physician schedule reliability. Record access controls build patient trust in the platform's privacy posture.","risk":"PRIMARY RISK: Unauthorized medical record access is a regulatory violation with severe penalties. SECONDARY RISK: Allowing unlicensed physicians to practice through the platform creates legal and patient-safety exposure. TERTIARY RISK: Race conditions in booking could undermine the double-booking invariant if not handled at the database level.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Directly affected by booking accuracy, cancellation flexibility, record privacy, and trust in physician licensing."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Schedule integrity protects their time. License verification is a compliance gate for onboarding. Note-locking protects clinical documentation integrity."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Bear responsibility for late cancellations and exception handling. Need clear tooling for override actions."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"License verification and record access controls are regulatory obligations. Failures here create institutional liability."},{"group":"Platform Operations","impact":"Must ensure infrastructure supports transactional booking integrity and reliable time-based triggers for note locking."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major business rules without contradiction. All six stated rules are unambiguous and map cleanly to testable assertions. Confidence is not higher because the spec does not address several operational details that would be needed for a complete implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention invariant is clearly defined","License verification as a prerequisite for booking is unambiguous","Cancellation window rules are explicit with clear role-based authority","Medical record access control rules are well-defined","Consultation note locking threshold is specific"],"low_confidence_areas":["Boundary condition for cancellation at exactly the 2-hour mark is not stated — assumed patient-favorable (still cancellable)","Whether treating physician access to records persists after the appointment or only during — spec says 'treating physician' without temporal scoping","Whether consultation notes can be created after the appointment and if so whether the 48-hour clock still runs from appointment end","What happens to existing bookings when a physician's license is revoked mid-schedule","Whether the 48-hour note lock can ever be overridden by an administrator or compliance officer"],"assumptions":["Appointment times are defined with both a start and end time, not just a start time plus fixed duration","The system uses server-side time for all time-based rules (cancellation window, note locking)","A treating physician is scoped to a specific appointment — not a general relationship","Staff Administrator is a distinct role, not a physician with extra privileges","Once consultation notes are locked, they are permanently immutable with no override mechanism"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
